answers: C++ is a powerful language used in game development, system programming, competitive programming, and performance-critical applications.
C++ basics: variables, pointers, arrays, OOP (classes, inheritance), STL (vectors, maps, sets), and memory management. Harder than Python but very rewarding.
Learn C++: start with basics on LearnCpp.com (free), practice on Codeforces or LeetCode. Great for competitive programming and understanding how computers work.
C vs C++: C is procedural (functions only), C++ adds OOP (classes, objects). C++ also has STL which makes coding much faster for competitive programming.
